notes: Verified via WebSearch 2026-07-11 (Wikipedia, TODAY.com, AOL, goldeagles.gold): Declaration of Independence adopted July 4, 1776 by the Second Continental Congress in Philadelphia; first anniversary celebration 1777; became an official federal holiday only in 1870.
USPS post offices fully closed with no regular mail delivery; banks/financial institutions/stock market closed as a federal holiday.

Deliberately did NOT hardcode any specific year (e.g. 2026) when describing the weekend-observance-shift rule — used the general evergreen rule instead ('if it falls on a weekend, official observance may shift to the nearest weekday') to avoid a stale/wrong fact next year.
Distinguished clearly from Canada Day (July 1) despite the close dates; content covers only US Independence Day facts.
Global/diaspora event (_countryCode: us), aliasSlugs: [] — same pattern as thanksgiving-usa/christmas-usa/canada-day.
